# PR Review Prompt

Review a pull request #{pr_number} in this repository, then post the findings as a GitHub review
with an overview body and inline comments. Mark everything as `[AI Generated]`.

---

## Step 1 — Discover the repository remote

```bash
git remote -v
```

Extract the `owner` and `repo` from the remote URL (e.g. `cs1-web-course-2026` / `cs14-minesweeper-2026`).

---

## Step 2 — Fetch PR metadata and diff

Use the GitHub API (or `gh` CLI / MCP tools) to retrieve in parallel:

- **PR details** — title, author login, head SHA, base branch, changed files count.
- **Full unified diff** — to read all added/modified/deleted lines.
- **File list** — to confirm which top-level folders are touched.

```bash
gh api /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr_number}
gh api /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr_number}/files
gh api /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr_number} --header "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3.diff"
```

---

## Step 3 — Review the diff against project conventions

Check every finding from the list below. Only report issues that are genuinely
impactful — bugs, accessibility failures, structural violations, logic errors.
Do **not** comment on style preferences or trivial formatting.

Follow `copilot-instructions.md`

---

## Step 4 — Build the review payload

Compose a JSON payload with the following structure:

```json
{
  "commit_id": "<head SHA from Step 2>",
  "body": "<overview — see format below>",
  "event": "REQUEST_CHANGES",
  "comments": [
    {
      "path": "<file path as it appears in the diff>",
      "line": <line number in the new file>,
      "side": "RIGHT",
      "body": "<inline comment — see format below>"
    }
  ]
}
```

Set `"event"` to:

- `"REQUEST_CHANGES"` — when there are critical or high-severity issues.
- `"COMMENT"` — when all issues are minor suggestions only.
- `"APPROVE"` — when the PR fully satisfies all checks.

### Overview body format

```markdown
## [AI Generated] Code Review

> This review was generated by GitHub Copilot.

### Summary

Short paragraph describing what the PR does and the overall quality.

- 🔴 **Critical — <title>:** one-line description.
- 🔴 **High — <title>:** one-line description.
- 🟠 **High — <title>:** one-line description.
- 🟡 **Medium — <title>:** one-line description.

Please address the critical and high issues before requesting re-review.
```

Severity scale:
| Emoji | Level | Examples |
|-------|-------|---------|
| 🔴 | Critical | Broken asset link, stylesheet not loading, corrupted output |
| 🔴 | High | Malformed HTML, folder name violation, deleted peer folder |
| 🟠 | High | Unclosed tags, logic errors |
| 🟡 | Medium | Accessibility, CSS specificity bug, naming violations, duplicate declarations |

### Inline comment body format

````markdown
🔴 **[AI Generated] <Category> — <Short title>**

<One paragraph explaining the problem, referencing the exact code.>

```<lang>
// Fix:
<corrected snippet>
```
````

---

## Step 5 — Post the review via `gh api`

```bash
gh api \
  --method POST \
  -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
  /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr_number}/reviews \
  --input /tmp/review_payload.json
```

Verify the response contains the expected `"state"` and that the inline comments
were attached by checking:

```bash
gh api /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr_number}/reviews/{review_id}/comments \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'Inline comments posted: {len(d)}')"
```

---

## Step 6 — Report back to the user

Summarise:

- Review URL (`html_url` from the API response).
- Review state (`CHANGES_REQUESTED` / `COMMENT` / `APPROVE`).
- Number of inline comments posted.
- A bullet list of all issues found, grouped by severity.
